That the Municipal Mayor require officials concerned to submit a copy of the duly perfected contracts/purchase orders, irrespective of amount, and all supporting documents thereto, to the audit team within five (5) working days from issuance thereof. The Purchase Order must be legible in all respects and should contain all the necessary data and information pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and paragraphs A (1) and A (3) of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

That the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor create strategies to intensify collection of Real Property Tax. Further, a notification letter should be sent to the Provincial Treasurer to request for the enforcement of possible remedies against delinquent taxpayers.
That the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the said report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
That the Municipal Accountant-designate revert back the stale checks ranging from two (2) years to eighteen (18) years in the books as debit to cash in bank and credit prior year’s adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer
(GSO) Designate to exert efforts to reconcile the PPE account of the
Municipality; and to maintain PPELC and PC respectively.
 Direct the GSO-Designate to provide complete unit value of the PPE
items listed in the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant:
 to submit the Bank Statements as supporting documents to the monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements;
 to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to record the reconciling
items duly supported with documentary evidence to validate adjustments
made;
 to analyze the difference between the bank reconciliation statement and
the Bank Information Inquiry to correct the deficiency of P 2,273,790.96;
and,
 to book up the savings account not recorded as of December 31, 2018 per
bank confirmation in the total amount of P 922,360.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to properly record expenses incurred for projects and PPEs so
that affected expense accounts be fairly reflected in the Municipality’s books
and financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the settlement of audit disallowances
and ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other
agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are
liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular 2009-006 that the
settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor
to expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made,
land ownership thru a certificate of title should be obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the submission of legal
basis in charging the project against the 20% DF contrary to the provisions of
DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated February
22, 2017.

Moreover, the Municipal Development Council and local officials should
ensure that the programs/projects included in the Municipality’s Annual
Investment Plan for the 20% DF are in consonance with the development
projects listed under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1
s. 2017 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to transfer all unexpended balance of LDRRMF as of year-end to the
prescribed Special Trust Fund account as required to protect the fund against
misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
develop an awareness program on the management of solid waste and choose
the appropriate means of communication in order for it to be effective.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type
of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to
assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans
for waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide technical support and
appropriate funds for the waste segregation at the barangay level; require the
SWM Focal Person to assign a personnel to inspect the waste to confirm the
type of waste to be disposed then direct the vehicle to the designated area
depending on the type of waste already inspected.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the SWM Focal
Person to implement a color coding or appropriate means of identification to
ensure that wastes are properly segregated at the residual containment area.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
maintain disposal site records in the sanitary residual containment area.

We recommended that the residual containment area be fenced with a secured
gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers on the area.
This will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by
limiting access.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the allocation of funds to
purchase the appropriate safety clothing as prescribed in the Manual for
Operation and Maintenance at Sanitary Landfill; the SWM Focal Person
should ensure that any person entering the area must wear the proper safety
clothing so that health risks are minimized and the operations at the RCA are
effective and safe.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere to the provision of Section 77 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
train and orient the personnel manning the VAW Help Desk on how to
respond and monitor violations committed against women.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the local
Sangunian work for an increased revenue by enacting appropriate ordinances
and monitor its implementation in order to collect more funds for the
barangay.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee provide reasonable
estimates of income in the annual budget based on historical data of the
Municipality to support development programs, projects and activities.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation and submission of periodic BRSs and to institute effective monitoring measures to ensure compliance thereof.  Also, to endeavor the Accountant to present to the depository banks the whereabouts of that long outstanding Deposit in Transits, with the help of the Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, determine those long outstanding checks and make the necessary adjustment by restoring  back the amounts to Cash in Bank Account as well as to its related accounting records.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to carefully monitor the reconciling items and make the necessary adjustment to reflect the balance Cash in Bank accounts and its related accounting records. Likewise, for the long outstanding Deposits in Transit, we advised the accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in finding the validated deposit slips and file appropriate complaints to concerned banks.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support establishing the reliability and validity of  accounts reported  in the Agency’s Financial Statements.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to set up periodic depreciation for all depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment as the regulation requires, so as to reflect accurate account balances.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to issue Demand Letters to above-enumerated officials or employees with outstanding unliquidated cash advances. Withholding of salary is also advised pursuant to existing rules and regulations. Lastly, establish effective monitoring measures to ensure proper granting and liquidation of cash advance consistent with the limitations sets for under the above-mentioned Decree and see to it that no cash advances remain unliquidated at the end of the year.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommend to require the Municipal Accountant to carefully evaluate the original entries of the two reciprocal accounts and remove those that cannot be validated through appropriate accounting entries.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries, as follows:
Dr. Deferred Real Property Tax			₱702,583.26
Cr. Real Property Tax Receivable					702,583.26
To record correcting entries

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to set up Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of each year based on the certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount due and collectible for the year to ensure that assets are correctly stated.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Agriculturist and Inventory Team to come up with duly reconciled records and reports pertaining to this account/property. Further, we advise the LGU to conduct inventory of assets periodically so as to reasonably substantiate financial records with actual  inventory.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that accounts are properly valued to reflect the true financial position of the LGU by reconciling its records with that of the Municipal assessor’s Office to ensure correctness of the recorded transactions and reliability of the account balances.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should secure proper documentation from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Assessor’s Office any relevant documents for the unbooked land properties to established reliability of the accounts to be recorded.
Finally, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to always uphold and protect her assertions on completeness, accuracy and valuation on the recorded transactions as well as on the fairness of the presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the land recorded as Investment Property to Land.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to oversee, as much as possible, the immediate transfer of the corresponding titles or tax declaration of those land properties subject in herein audit in the name of the municipal government as the registered owner. In so doing, this would do away the burden of former owners from taxes for those portion already sold to the Municipal Government.

RECOMMENDATION:
In view hereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineering, BAC,  BAC Secretariat and other municipal official/s directly responsible the submission of the above-enumerated TSO-required documents, to submit immediately with the auditing office the lacking documents, so as to facilitate for complete technical review and evaluation of the subject contracts.  Henceforth and for future similar undertakings, please see to it that within five (5) working days from the execution of contract, a copy of said contract along with supporting documents, as outlined in the said COA Circulars, forming part thereof shall be furnished to this Audit Team within this prescribed time.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended the  Municipal Treasurer to draw cash advances on a  per transaction basis to provide easy analysis of the transactions.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to request the Municipal Accountant to record the completed projects in the appropriate asset accounts in order to reflect the correct balances. Moving forward, we advise the Municipal Engineer to provide Certificate of Completion and/or Certificates of Turnover to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the accounting records reflect the accurate financial position of the LGU.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer thru the Social Pension Focal Person to submit amended or corrected certified list of beneficiaries to avoid inconsistency. We also advise the management to carefully monitor succeeding grants of social pension and other benefits for the welfare of the community to ensure that only the targeted beneficiaries receive the benefits due to them. Lastly, we strongly recommend that, in cases where the target beneficiaries cannot personally get their claims, require a written authorizations to be presented otherwise the release should be put on hold.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended the municipal government through the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Development Officer and other concern to compel the contractors to expedite the completion of the projects.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require the BAC Secretariat to submit the above-listed documents to facilitate the conduct of auditorial and technical review and evaluation of the contract including the inspection of deliveries. Further, for future similar responsivity, please see to it to observe all the procurement processes in the competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and to submit completely the lacking  documents to avoid delay in the review and evaluation processes of contract.
More further, assign additional personnel to assist the official in charge in the submission of contract documents to the Audit Team particularly in the review/checking on its completeness.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended to require  the BAC Secretariat to  submit the perfected contract and other supporting documents within time frame in order to facilitate review and evaluation thereof to avoid sanction herein stated.

Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees
with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its
obligations. The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the amounts
withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability
of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human
Resource Management Officer or other concerned officials and employees to device
a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of
contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.

The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an
Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No. 4177 and AO 70 to give
assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the
Municipality.

We recommended to:
1. Require the concerned officers and employees to liquidate/settle their
respective outstanding cash advances by:
 Demanding the immediate submission of duly accomplish liquidation
reports;
 Preparing final demand letter to be issued to concerned officers
and employees accountable of the cash advances that remained
unsettled for more than 60 days;
 Otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of their salaries
for non-compliance therewith.
2. Strengthen the monitoring controls on cash advances to ensure that
these are liquidated within prescribed period

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to carefully determine
collectivity of those mentioned accounts and undertake rigorous follow up on the
submission of liquidation reports, otherwise file for request for authority to write-off
dormant unliquidated cash advances with Commission on Audit on manner and
procedures prescribed under above-mentioned COA Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommended to:
1. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a thorough and complete physical
inventory of all properties owned by the Municipal Government. To facilitate
the inventory taking, the MTGSO may require each office to conduct physical
count of PPEs under their custody and prepare an inventory report that is
complete in all particulars, such as, item description, model, serial number and
property numbers, acquisition costs and dates, for submission to the Inventory
Committee who in turn validate the submitted inventory reports and come up
with the consolidated RPCPPE.
2. Likewise, endeavor to reconcile the balance of said report with accounting
records and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections to ensure the
correctness of valuation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to comply with this
particular requirement is highly recommended with the cost thereof be incorporated in the
Program of Work.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the completion of
the said project not only to satisfy the provisions of the agreement, but likewise to have
the services of said project available to the intended beneficiaries at much needed time.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to demand for the
extension/renewal of the validity of the performance security posted to those ongoing
projects with extended contract time, so as to ensure full coverage of the project up to its
final acceptance.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer, and the other representatives
of the Municipal Planning Development Office to ensure that all necessary items to be
done are well planned to avoid the non-implementation/delays of the programmed
activities chargeable to GF and 20% LDP.

We recommended to require Municipal management should strictly compel the
suppliers to properly acknowledge the issuance of approved Purchase Order by indicating
the date of receipt of such document.

We recommended to require the BAC Secretariat to make strong representation
with DA management for the issuance of CAP and accordingly institute effective
monitoring system, so as to fast-track the implementation of the projects.

We recommended to:
1. Require the Human Resource Officer to consider filling up those vacant
positions including may be creating relevant and necessary position/s or offices
for potential improvement of operations as well as on the efficiencies and
effectiveness of services to the public.
2. Consider reviewing/revisiting its existing structure and manpower vis-à-
vis realistic needs in order to maximize operational efficiency.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should install
adequate measure/s to check time-to-time the accountability of its designated revenue
collectors to ensure that collections for the period are properly reported and fully
remitted.

We recommended that management, Municipal Accountant, ensure that all supporting documents were already attached to succeeding procurements relative to the procurement conduct of public bidding from the planning stage up to the issuance of the Notice to Proceed should be attached to their respective DVs.

We strongly recommended the identification of the Capital Outlay, which purpose has already been completed and the identified portion to facilitate transfer to the MOOE portion of the unobligated balance both in the prior years 2016 and current year immediately transfer the identified amount in a Special Trust Fund.

items to correct the amount for the Cash in Bank account and to inform the
depository Bank of the reconciling items requiring adjustments in the books of the
bank.

a) Require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of
Section 111 of PD 1445, and Section 491 of the GAAM Vol. I, in order
to adequately furnish the accurate information of the PPE of the LGU.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant-designate and the General Supply
Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to reconcile the
difference between the balances per books with the physical count as of
December 31, 2016 in order to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE
account.

small tangible items with estimated useful life of more than one year

issuance, per Items 2.2 of COA Circular 2005-002 dated April 14,

and Regulations of RA 9184.
Require the BAC to submit justification on why the delivery of
bamboo and fruit trees seedlings was made ahead of the notice to
proceed, purchase order and contract of agreement.

We recommended that the LCE :
a) Require the personnel assigned in the preparation of the purchase order to
observe COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to indicate all the
necessary information specifically the specifications of the goods to be
purchased, and to ensure that the goods delivered meet the specification
within the time frame.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check the propriety of the
Purchase Order and not to pass for payment those claims with incomplete
information to avoid suspensions in audit.

section 37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e., to post in the PhilGEPS the notice of award of
contract, notice to proceed, and approved contract of agreements for transparency
and information in all procurement processes.

Section 21.2.1 (a) of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA
9184, and should require the Bids and Awards Committee to publish the Invitation
to Bid with an ABC of five (5) million and above in the newspaper of general
circulation which has been regularly published for at least two (2) years before the
date of issue of the advertisement.

submit Fund Utilization Reports upon the completion of the project.

the Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the project
must be supported by inspection report of the Municipal Engineer upon
100% completion.

Education Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, programs and activities
mandated in Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

should strictly adhere to the required payment of checks directly to payees thru

being the most contradicted ones. Furthermore, the concerned official should plan

years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in the LDRRMFIP.

necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to

ecological solid waste management program; (d) strictly comply with all other
prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
